message:
feat(core,planner,vdbe): enforce UNIQUE constraints in MemDatabase and lift INSERT/UPDATE expression restrictions

Three related correctness improvements:

1. UNIQUE constraint enforcement in MemDatabase: MemTable now tracks
   groups of column indices that form UNIQUE constraints (both column-
   level UNIQUE/non-IPK PRIMARY KEY and table-level composite UNIQUE).
   The Insert opcode checks for conflicts before inserting, and
   OE_REPLACE correctly deletes the conflicting row before upserting.
   This was invisible with real B-tree storage (indexes enforce it) but
   caused silent duplicate rows in Phase 4 in-memory mode.

2. INSERT/UPDATE expression support: codegen previously rejected any
   non-literal, non-placeholder values in INSERT VALUES and UPDATE SET
   clauses. Now all expressions (arithmetic, function calls, CASE, CAST,
   column references, etc.) are routed through emit_expr, matching
   SQLite's behavior.

3. INSERT DEFAULT VALUES: was a hard error; now emits proper codegen
   that allocates a rowid and inserts a row with all-NULL columns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
